commit.message:
fix: remove lexical from peerDependencies — bundled as dependency

lexical is an internal implementation detail. Users never touch the
Lexical API directly (no public types, no exported Lexical instances).
Making it a peer was wrong — it required users to install an extra
package they never use.

- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/core
- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/plugins
- Update install instructions in all READMEs and docs

Users now only need:
  pnpm add @lexify/core

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix stale dashboard 404 after "Reset All State" (#1814)

Resetting all state while on `/dashboards/:id` preserved the URL, so
reconnecting would attempt to load a now-nonexistent dashboard and
immediately show a 404.

- **`App.tsx`**: Navigate to `/dashboards` (with `replace`) after
`resetState()` in the reset confirmation handler
- **`App.test.tsx`**: Add test covering the
reset-from-stale-dashboard-URL → `/dashboards` navigation

Issue #2 in the report (auth tab state retained between connection
types) is intentional—credentials persist across tab switches as a
convenience UX pattern and are cleared on dialog close or full state
reset.

- Fixes elastic/ai-github-actions-playground#1800

Add auto-update via electron-updater

- Install electron-updater and wire it into the main process
- On startup (production only), check GitHub Releases for new
  versions, auto-download, and install on quit
- Add IPC channels: updater:check (manual), updater:install
  (quit & install), updater:status (push notifications)
- Add update UI in Settings: shows download progress, 'Install
  vX.Y.Z' button when ready, or manual 'Check for updates'
- Configure electron-builder publish target to github provider
  so update manifests (latest.yml) are generated in releases
- Add electron-updater to webpack externals

Release flow: tag a version → CI builds .exe + latest.yml →
users get auto-updated on next launch.

commit.message:
fix: pass model ID to Anthropic chat clients to prevent 'model: Field required' errors (#125)

Both ClaudeCodeDetector and AnthropicDetector created AnthropicPromptCachingChatClient
without setting the model ID in ChatOptions. The Anthropic API requires the model field,
causing 'invalid_request_error: model: Field required' or empty responses.

Fix: wrap the chat client in ChatClientBuilder.ConfigureOptions(o => o.ModelId ??= modelId)
matching the pattern already used in the connector's UseClaudeCodeChatCompletion extension.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix filter bar button height mismatch in LogsSearchPanel (#1817)

## Summary

The reported toolbar height mismatch was attributed to Traces, but this
fix addresses the actual mismatch in `LogsSearchPanel`. In the Logs
filter toolbar rows, `TextField size="small"` controls render taller
than neighboring small buttons, so button minimum heights are aligned to
the shared input token.

## Changes

- Added `minHeight: COMPONENT_HEIGHTS.input` to these `LogsSearchPanel`
buttons that sit beside small text inputs:
  - `Apply`
  - `Run pivot`
  - `Add service filter`
  - `Add host filter`
  - `Add level filter`
- Reused the existing tokenized height approach (`minHeight`, not fixed
`height`) so theme touch-target overrides remain intact.
- Fixes elastic/ai-github-actions-playground#1801

## Checklist

- [ ] Ran `make check` successfully (lint + unit tests + build)
- [ ] Included a screenshot if I made a UX change
- [ ] Updated documentation if applicable

---
The body of this PR [is automatically
managed](https://ela.st/github-ai-tools) by the [Update PR Body
workflow](https://github.com/elastic/ai-github-actions-playground/actions/runs/22701267377).

https://github.com/elastic/ai-github-actions-playground/actions/runs/22701267377
-->

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: strawgate <6384545+strawgate@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix Metrics page: truncated empty state, blank chart panel, inconsistent toolbar heights (#1816)

Exploration bot flagged four defects on the Metrics page. Three are real
bugs; one (combobox no-results state) is working as designed.

### Truncated empty state description

`EmptyState` description `Typography` had `noWrap` + `overflow: hidden`,
truncating text like "Pick a namespace to see an overview of all its
...". Removed both — text now wraps naturally within its `maxWidth: 320`
container.

### Blank chart panel when metric selected but no data

`ExploreContentArea` had no fallback for the state where a metric is
selected but `chartData` is null (query idle, error, or empty result).
The panel rendered completely blank. Added a "No chart data"
`EmptyState` guarded by `selectedMetric && !chartData && queryStatus !==
"loading"`.

### Toolbar element height inconsistency

Applied `COMPONENT_HEIGHTS.input` (36px) explicitly to Namespace
`Select`, Search metrics `Autocomplete` input, Index pattern
`TextField`, and Aggregation `Select` — matching the pattern used
elsewhere (e.g. `RequestCard`, `ProfilingToolbar`). The global
`MuiOutlinedInput` theme override should handle this, but explicit
heights provide defense-in-depth for components like Autocomplete that
layer additional styling.

### Search combobox no-results state (no change)

Reporter expected icon + title + description for empty search results,
but this is a standard MUI Autocomplete `noOptionsText` dropdown — the
reporter's own note suggests the expectation was misaligned.

- Fixes elastic/ai-github-actions-playground#1802

commit.message:
fix: hide window immediately before Ctrl+V inject to fix paste timing race (v1.4.9)

_animate_hide() takes 200ms but previous settle_ms was 150ms, so Ctrl+V
fired while CyberClip was still the foreground window, sending Ctrl+V to
our own search box instead of the target app. Fix: self.hide() immediately
(synchronous) before scheduling the inject with 200ms settle.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix Azure Web App startup timeout: disable production demo seeding and pin Kestrel port (#377) Azure App Service was timing out (10 min readiness probe) because the app ran heavy demo seeding before `app.Run()` and had no explicit port binding configured. ## Changes - **`appsettings.Production.json`** — explicitly sets `DemoSeed:Enabled: false` - `GetDemoSeedEnabled()` falls back to `true` for SQLite when the key is absent, causing 25 tickets to be processed through the full pipeline on every cold start - **`deploy-azure.yml`** — adds `ASPNETCORE_URLS=http://+:8080` to the `az webapp config appsettings set` call - Aligns Kestrel's bind address with Azure's readiness probe port and the `EXPOSE 8080` already declared in the `Dockerfile`
Original prompt > > ---- > > *This section details on the original issue you should resolve* > > [CI Failure Doctor] Azure web app fails to start within 10 minutes > ### CI Failure Investigation - Run samuelkahessay/prd-to-prod#82 > > ### Summary > `deploy-azure / build-and-deploy` completes restore/build/test/publish, but the Azure deployment step never reaches a running worker: App Service keeps logging "Starting the site" for over 10 minutes, then marks the worker as failed. > > ### Failure Details > - **Run**: [22697236375](https://github.com/samuelkahessay/prd-to-prod/actions/runs/22697236375) > - **Commit**: 23caa053ec81bd2f7d497a2ce3cc3b3a4135f266 > - **Trigger**: push > > ### Root Cause Analysis > The failure occurs inside the `Deploy to Azure Web App` step. Azure logs repeatedly report `Status: Starting the site...` followed by `Site failed to start within 10 mins` / `worker process failed to start within the allotted time`, which means the container never became HTTP-ready. The app still seeds demo data on every startup (since `DemoSeed:Enabled` defaults to `true` for the SQLite provider) and runs tens of EF operations before `app.Run()`, so a production startup could easily block the readiness probe for several minutes. Another possibility is that the app is binding to a different port than App Service is probing (no explicit `ASPNETCORE_URLS` is set and the zipped app may still be listening on the default port), so Azure never gets a response. > > ### Failed Jobs and Errors > - `deploy-azure / build-and-deploy` – `Deploy to Azure Web App` exits with `ERROR: Deployment failed because the site failed to start within 10 mins.`; logs finally report `Status: Site failed to start. Time: 616(s)` and `Error: Deployment failed because the worker process failed to start within the allotted time.` > >
> Investigation Findings > > - The build/test/publish steps succeed, so the failure is limited to the App Service runtime. > - Azure waited the entire 10-minute health window while the worker kept logging `Status: Starting the site...` (see log lines near 01:15:56 UTC). > - Startup currently runs the demo seeding pipeline because the SQLite provider remains enabled in production; the seed touches many EF entities and may be blocking Kestrel from answering the first HTTP request quickly enough for App Service to consider the worker ready. > - The zipped deployment does not explicitly set `ASPNETCORE_URLS` / `WEBSITES_PORT`, so if the app binds to a different port than the one App Service probes, the worker never reports healthy despite the process running. >
> > ### Recommended Actions > - [ ] Pull the Azure runtime logs ((prdtoprod.scm.azurewebsites.net/redacted) to capture the exact exception or readiness failure that kept the worker from logging a successful start. > - [ ] Turn off the demo seeding path in production (`DemoSeed:Enabled=false` in production config or guarded by an environment flag) so the startup routine cannot monopolize the first several minutes. > - [ ] Ensure Kestrel listens on the port App Service expects (set `ASPNETCORE_URLS` / `WEBSITES_PORT` or pass `--urls (redacted) then redeploy and confirm `Deploy to Azure Web App` finishes successfully. > > ### Prevention Strategies > Limit heavy initialization work on startup and keep production seeding gated; also verify any environment that deploys to Azure App Service is configured to listen on the expected port so the 10-minute readiness probe never times out. > > ### AI Team Self-Improvement > When adding startup seed/migration logic, default it off for production and simulate App Service startup (including the expected listening port and HTTP probe) locally so CI catches any readiness slowness earlier. > >
> Historical Context > > No previous `[CI Failure Doctor]` issue has documented this App Service startup timeout; the most recent issues (#358 and samuelkahessay/prd-to-prod#360) were navigation-test mismatches unrelated to deployment runtime. >
> > > > > > 🩺 *Diagnosis provided by [CI Failure Doctor](https://github.com/samuelkahessay/prd-to-prod/actions/runs/22697574700)* > > - [x] expires on Mar 6, 2026, 1:23 AM UTC > > > > > > > ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section) > > > >
- Fixes samuelkahessay/prd-to-prod#376 --- 💬 We'd love your input! Share your thoughts on Copilot coding agent in our [2 minute survey](https://gh.io/copilot-coding-agent-survey). --------- Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> Co-authored-by: samuelkahessay <28946220+samuelkahessay@users.noreply.github.com>

Feature batch: split components, streaming markdown, auto-title, shortcuts, and more

- Split ThreadPanel (1050 lines) into ModelPicker, DiffView, and
  TerminalView as separate component files
- Render markdown incrementally during streaming instead of only
  after completion
- Auto-title threads from the first user message content
- Add keyboard shortcuts: Ctrl/Cmd+N (new thread), Ctrl/Cmd+,
  (settings), Ctrl/Cmd+L (focus chat input)
- Wire up sidebar ListFilter button with thread search/filter
- Reuse existing CopilotClient for listModels instead of creating
  a throwaway client each call
- Git instance cache: switch from FIFO to LRU eviction so active
  projects stay cached
- Thread cleanup: prune persisted threads to 100 max, 50 messages
  each, preventing unbounded localStorage growth
- Add ARIA labels to sidebar nav, tab buttons, and chat input

commit.message:
Add 12->3 octahedral result (silver ratio), fill 11->3 gap, fix f-vectors

  b/a = 1+sqrt(2) (silver ratio), a^2 = (5-2*sqrt(2))/17. Volume 13.5096.
- 11->3: symmetry order 2, not equilateral, volume 11.744
- Fix 12->3 f-vector: (134, 264, 132) not (200, 396, 198)

commit.message:
feat: Create WonderGolf — browser-based daily miniature golf game

Single-file HTML5/CSS3/vanilla-JS mini-golf game (index.html):

Core gameplay
- Click-and-drag slingshot putting (drag away from target, release to shoot)
- Animated aim line with dashed trajectory, arrowhead, rubber-band indicator
- Power bar drawn on canvas during aiming
- Ball physics: rolling friction, wall bounce off polygon segment normals,
  multiple-pass collision resolution, cup sink detection
- Sinking animation; auto-advance at par+4 strokes

Course & holes
- 12 pre-built holes: straight, L-shape, Z/dogleg, T-shape, chicane, island
- Daily 9-hole round seeded by date via mulberry32 PRNG + Fisher-Yates shuffle
- Polygon boundary walls + interior obstacle segments

Hazards
- Sand traps (rect + circle): high friction (0.90/frame)
- Water hazards (rect + circle): immediate +1 penalty + reset to last safe pos
- Slick zones (rect + circle): low friction (0.996/frame)
- Animated water ripples, sand stipple dots

Scoring & persistence
- Score labels: Hole in One, Eagle, Birdie, Par, Bogey, Double Bogey, +N
- XP system (10 levels) + 6 achievements stored in localStorage
- Full scorecard after 9 holes with color-coded per-hole results

UI screens
- Main menu with animated demo hole in canvas background
- In-game HUD (hole/par/strokes/total drawn on canvas)
- Hole complete card with score badge + XP gained
- Final scorecard table
- Profile screen with level, XP bar, achievement grid

Technical
- Responsive: 800×540 logical canvas, CSS-scaled to any viewport
- Touch + mouse input
- No build step, no dependencies

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Agent: Project Organization Sweep (#7494)

* Agent: Project Organization Sweep

* Initial plan

* Move annotate-static-overrides from refactor workspace to lint workspace

The transform `annotateStaticFunctionOverridesTransform` previously lived
in `src/refactor/src/annotate-static-overrides.ts` but had no connection
to the refactor domain (rename/restructuring transactions). Its sole
purpose is to set `_overridesStaticFunction = true` on AST nodes whose
corresponding property is exclusively read by the lint workspace's
`synthetic-comments.ts` to emit `@override` doc-comment tags.

Because the lint workspace never imports from `@gml-modules/refactor`, the
transform was effectively orphaned: defined but never called, making the
`_overridesStaticFunction` branch in `synthetic-comments.ts` permanently
dead.

New location: `src/lint/src/rules/gml/transforms/comments/annotate-static-overrides.ts`
Old location: `src/refactor/src/annotate-static-overrides.ts`

Changes:
- Moved the file to the lint `transforms/comments/` directory, co-located
  with `synthetic-comments.ts` which consumes its output
- Removed the lone `as any` cast; uses `MutableGameMakerAstNode & { id?: unknown }`
- Exported from `comments/index.ts` and `transforms/index.ts` barrels
- Registered as the first entry in `TRANSFORM_REGISTRY_ENTRIES` with an
  ordering comment explaining why it must precede `stripCommentsTransform`
- Added 9 unit tests in `src/lint/test/annotate-static-overrides.test.ts`
  using a proper `AnnotatedNode` type rather than `as any` assertions

Co-authored-by: henrylkirk <15679826+henrylkirk@users.noreply.github.com>

* Reduce cognitive complexity in collectConstructorInfos by extracting helpers

Extract resolveConstructorName, resolveParentConstructorName, and
collectStaticFunctions so collectConstructorInfos stays below the
sonarjs/cognitive-complexity threshold of 15.

Co-authored-by: henrylkirk <15679826+henrylkirk@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: prettier-plugin-gml
repository.description: Adds Game Maker Language (GML) language support to Prettier

commit.message:
Agent: Target State Alignment (#7492)

* Agent: Target State Alignment

* Initial plan

* format: remove printBooleanReturnIf — enforce formatter/linter architectural boundary

The formatter was performing a semantic content rewrite in printBooleanReturnIf:
detecting `if (cond) { return true; } else { return false; }` and transforming
it to `return cond;` (and the negated variant to `return !cond;`).

This violates target-state.md §2.2 / §3.2: "Format must not perform
semantic/content rewrites — those belong in the lint workspace."

The transformation is already owned by @gml-modules/lint's
gml/optimize-logical-flow rule (condensation.ts:resolveSimpleBooleanReturnArgument
and traversal-normalization.ts:simplifyIfStatement), so duplicating it in the
formatter was a direct violation of the two-surface ownership model.

Changes:
- Remove printBooleanReturnIf, getBooleanReturnBranch,
  getBooleanReturnStatementInfo, negateExpressionDoc, needsParensForNegation
  from print.ts; IfStatement now calls buildIfStatementDoc directly
- Remove unused constants (TERNARY_EXPRESSION, LOGICAL_EXPRESSION,
  BINARY_EXPRESSION) that were only referenced by the removed functions
- Update test-flow.output.gml fixtures to show full if/else bodies
- Add two contract tests in formatter-boundaries-ownership.test.ts documenting
  that boolean-return simplification is lint-only behavior

Co-authored-by: henrylkirk <15679826+henrylkirk@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(python): add timeout parameter to generated RPC methods (#681)

* fix(python): add timeout parameter to generated RPC methods

Every generated async RPC method now accepts an optional `timeout`
keyword argument that is forwarded to `JsonRpcClient.request()`.
This lets callers override the default 30s timeout for long-running
RPCs like `session.fleet.start` without bypassing the typed API.

Fixes #539

* test: cover no-params and server-scoped RPC timeout branches

Add tests for PlanApi.read (session, no params) and ModelsApi.list
(server, no params) to exercise all four codegen branches.

* fix: move _timeout_kwargs after quicktype imports, add server+params test

- Move _timeout_kwargs helper after the quicktype-generated import block
  to avoid duplicate Optional import and keep preamble conventional
- Add ToolsApi.list tests covering the server-scoped + params branch
- All four codegen branches now have test coverage

* style: fix ruff format in test_rpc_timeout.py

* fix: use float | None instead of Optional[float] in generated RPC

Optional is not imported in the generated rpc.py file. On Python 3.11
(used in CI), annotations are eagerly evaluated, so Optional[float]
would cause NameError at import time. Use the modern float | None
union syntax which requires no import and matches the rest of the
generated code.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: wrap long docstring line in client.py to pass ruff E501

Pre-existing ruff line-length violation in get_last_session_id docstring
example that was failing CI on main.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: resolve pre-existing CI failures in docs and ruff lint

- docs/guides/custom-agents.md: use typed enum constants instead of
  string literals for PermissionRequestResultKind (C# and Go examples)
- python/copilot/client.py: wrap long docstring line to satisfy E501

Both issues pre-exist on main and block all PR CI runs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Rasaboun <40967731+Rasaboun@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(ux): add actionable guidance to error messages

- SessionCreationFailed: suggest checking project path
- SessionSwitchFailed: suggest list_sessions
- WorkerCommunicationFailed: suggest hard_reset_fsi_session
- WorkerSpawnFailed: suggest checking .NET SDK and dotnet build
- WorkerTimeout: suggest retry or hard_reset
- PipeClosed: explain crash recovery with hard_reset
- ResetFailed: suggest hard_reset escalation
- HardResetFailed: suggest checking dotnet build
- ScriptLoadFailed: suggest checking file existence and syntax
- HotReloadFailed: suggest checking syntax errors
- RestartLimitExceeded: suggest checking logs and restarting

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: consolidate duplicate image and match utility functions

Extract common image manipulation functions (FillRect, FillCircle, Abs)
into new pkg/common/imgutil package used across sprite, weapon, itemicon,
and loot systems. Extract match respawn logic into pkg/network/matchutil
to eliminate duplication between FFAMatch and TeamMatch implementations.

- Create pkg/common/imgutil with shared image utilities and tests
- Create pkg/network/matchutil with PlayerState interface and adapters
- Remove duplicate implementations from sprite_gen, weapon/sprite_gen,
  ai/sprite_gen, itemicon/system, loot/visual_system, hazard/system
- Update all callers to use consolidated functions
- Reduce codebase by 514 lines while improving maintainability

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
